text.title: Email Preflight Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Email Preflight Checklist. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every send runs all 12 items; skipped items require a one-line reason logged with the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: Silent skips are the dominant checklist-rot mode.

rule.statement: Each item names its source-of-truth (system, dashboard, URL). Items without a named source are deleted as speculation.
rule.rationale: Measurement without a defined gauge is not measurement.

rule.statement: Owner is a named person (handle), never 'team' / 'we' / 'everyone'. Plural ownership is no ownership.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership leads to no ownership — sends ship broken.

rule.statement: Cap the preflight at 30 min wall-clock. Items that don't fit get split or deferred, never crammed.
rule.rationale: Over-budget checklists are abandoned by cycle 4.

rule.statement: If any item fails red, the send is blocked. No 'send anyway with caveat'.
rule.rationale: Soft blocks normalise red-state sends and erode the gate.

rule.statement: Each 'done' item attaches an evidence link (URL, screenshot, query). No evidence = not done.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-email-preflight-checklist.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
